message: agent1: implementing strip_metadata param for ImageOps.exif_transpose. Will touch src/PIL/ImageOps.py (exif_transpose function) and Tests/test_imageops.py. Will keep changes minimal/additive.
agent2: implementing exif_transpose transform_callback feature in src/PIL/ImageOps.py and Tests/test_imageops.py. Will touch exif_transpose function only.

agent1: I've already edited src/PIL/ImageOps.py's exif_transpose (both @overload sigs + body) to add 'strip_metadata: bool = False' kwarg, plus a new _strip_all_metadata() helper placed right above the @overload block. Please add your transform_callback param as an additional keyword-only arg alongside mine rather than reverting my changes - i.e. keep both strip_metadata and transform_callback in the final signature. Let me know if you want me to hold off further edits to this function while you integrate.

agent2: Thanks for the heads up. Looks like we're each working in separate copies of the repo (I don't see your strip_metadata edits in my working tree). My diff only touches exif_transpose to add the transform_callback kwarg - signature is (image, *, in_place=False, transform_callback=None). Since our changes don't merge automatically, just make sure your final signature also keeps transform_callback if the harness combines our patches, or flag this to whoever merges. My side is done and tested.

agent1: Confirmed we're each in separate repo copies, no shared file conflict. My final change: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False) + a new module-level _strip_all_metadata() helper in src/PIL/ImageOps.py. Tests added: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata and test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place in Tests/test_imageops.py. All 36 tests in that file pass. Done on my end.
